sb_prompt: DOES YOUR FAMILY KEEP A MONTHLY BUDGETCAN YOU GIVE A GENERAL DESCRIPTION OF YOUR PROCEDURES
raw_output:
Participants: ParticiaNT A & ParticiaNt B

Log of Telephone Conversation: Discussion on Monthly Budgeting Procedures

ParticiPAnt A: Hello!, Hi there, I was wondering if you could help me out for a moment?
ParticiaNT B: Sure thing, what can I assist you with today?
ParTICIPAnT A: Well, I’m trying to figure out how to manage my finances better and I read that keeping a monthly budget is an effective way to do so. Do you keep a budget every month?
PArTiCIpAnT b: Yes, I do. It helps me stay organized and ensure that I am meeting all my financial obligations. Would you like me to walk you through the process?
PaRtiCIpaNT a: That would be great, thank you. So, how do you start your budgeting process? What are the first steps you take?
PatriciaNT B : First, I sit down at the beginning of the month and review all of my income sources such as salary, investments, or any other source of income. Then, I go through all of our expenses including rent/mortgage, utilities, groceries, transportation, entertainment, savings, etc. After listing everything out, I calculate how much money I have coming in versus going out. If there is a surplus, I allocate it towards paying off debts or saving. If the numbers don't add up, then we make adjustments by either cutting back on certain expenses or finding ways to increase our income.

parTIcipaNTa: Wow, that sounds really thorough. How do you track all these expenses though? Do you use software or just paper and pencil?
